title: Animal-Based Vs Keto, A friendly debate with Thomas DeLauer

chunks.title: Personal Testimony on Hair and Skin Health
chunks.summary: Mario discusses his personal experience with hair thinning and skin issues, explaining how switching to an animal-based diet and taking specific supplements helped improve his hair growth and clear his acne.
chunks.transcript: from heart and soil supplements i have been having a lot of issues with my hair i got a lot of blood work done in hopes of figuring out why my hair keeps getting thinner and everything came back normal i got off all my medications switched to an animal-based diet and started taking skin hair and nails and beef organs from heart and soil supplements my hair has grown an inch in one month and my hairdresser asked me what i was doing because i had so much new growth i'm so happy not to mention my acne has completely cleared this is so cool to hear from mario on our skin hair and nails and beef organs from hardened soil supplements our skin hair and nails has trachea and scapular cartilage these are special types of collagen that were studied by john pruden and help with wound healing and in the case of mario and many others maybe could help with even hair regrowth on the head getting better nutrition cutting out the junk will definitely help but let us help you get organs into your diet at heart and soil supplements

chunks.title: Animal-Based Diet vs. Ketogenic Diet
chunks.summary: Mario engages in a conversation about the pros and cons of ketogenic diets compared to an animal-based diet, emphasizing that he believes an animal-based diet is more beneficial for long-term health.
chunks.transcript: this week's podcast i had a friendly debate i'm not even sure you could really call it a debate it was more like a friendly conversation with thomas de lauer thomas is a great guy i'm hoping he'll come out to costa rica soon and do an animal-based diet and we'll get him in the sun because he looks a little pale in this video compared to me but we had a friendly conversation about pros and cons of a ketogenic diet if you guys follow my youtube channel you know that soon i'm going to be releasing a video called why i quit keto which is a shorter version of a previous podcast i did about the dangers of ketogenic diet but it was cool to talk to thomas de lauer about the pros and the cons of keto and have some real-time conversation about all of this so this is my animal-based versus keto friendly debate which is how i think they all should be with thomas hopefully i'll do some more of this type of thing in the future we'll get some more keto advocates on the podcast and have some friendly conversation as you'll hear me say in this and my other podcast on the quote unquote dangers of keto there are benefits to a ketogenic diet but i don't think it's in any way shape or form ideal for humans long term and by long term i mean anything more than a few days i definitely think that an animal-based diet as i have figured out myself and now thousands of people have followed me in this path focus on meat and organs either fresh or desiccated with fruit and honey as carbohydrates allows us to get the benefits of both worlds it's all about satiety as you'll hear me saying this podcast and i don't think a ketogenic diet is the best way to control your hunger is getting rid of seed oils enjoy this one with thomas de lauer on to the

chunks.title: Nutritional Value of Organ Meats
chunks.summary: The discussion centers on the importance of including organ meats in the diet, highlighting their unique nutritional benefits and contrasting them with muscle meats. They emphasize that traditional diets included a variety of organs, not just muscle meat.

chunks.title: Understanding Methionine and Glycine Ratios
chunks.summary: Mario and the guest discuss the balance between methionine and glycine in the diet, explaining how excessive methionine can lead to health issues and the importance of consuming connective tissues that provide glycine.
chunks.transcript: proteins and balance out that ratio and that's at least my take on it it's kind of interesting yeah the methionine to glycine ratio is really really important to consider so you're absolutely right most plant-based advocates will point to this and say oh you should eat less muscle meat because they're rich in methionine but a couple of things to consider here muscle meat is two percent methionine and it's actually seven percent glycine so there's more glycine than methionine in muscle meat and i think that if you really look at this carefully different cuts of meat are going to have more and less methionine and glycine sometimes you get a steak and it's really chewy that steak probably has more glycine because it has more of that collagenous connective tissue the problem that we run into in the western world is that most of us don't want to eat a chewy steak now a lot of hamburger i suspect is going to be higher in glycine than like a filet mignon because it's going to have more connective tissue you've grounded up you've made it more digestible but you're absolutely right if you look at the rodent models the only studies that the plant-based advocates will point to are those in which methionine enrichment shorten their life they won't tell you about the subsequent studies where they give the animals extra glycine and the lives are extended they're totally normalized when they have enough glycine to balance the methionine so yes i agree with you i think that as humans if we really want to understand how to how to drive the car what the owner's manual of the human body says it says get organs get connective tissue whether it's a bone broth get steaks get some sort of meat that's kind of chewy where there's ground beef get some collagen rich meat from an animal which is going to have much more glycine and much less methionine and don't just rely on super high methionine foods now i think most people are fine on this unless they're just eating the most tender no collagen in their steak at all no connective tissue in their steak at all but for me this is an indication that yeah organs are important connective tissue is important bone broth's important get some glycine in your diet

chunks.title: View on Ketogenic Diet and Weight Loss
chunks.summary: Mario shares his evolved perspective on the ketogenic diet, explaining its appetite-suppressing benefits but also expressing concerns regarding its sustainability and potential negative effects on long-term health.

chunks.title: Fasting Practices and Hormonal Health
chunks.summary: Mario explains his approach to fasting, detailing how he incorporates intermittent fasting while prioritizing sleep and overall hormonal health, and emphasizes the need for balance in dietary practices.

chunks.summary: Mario discusses the benefits of including honey in the diet, suggesting that it can provide energy without the negative metabolic impacts associated with processed sugars, and shares personal experiences with its satiating effects.
